ideation: This sequence mimics gently offering a hibiscus flower. It starts with arms down, moves to a presentation pose, and returns to rest. The movement is slow and deliberate, emphasizing the delicate nature of the flower. The left arm will remain mostly still to create visual balance and focus on the offering movement of the right arm. In the first frame, both arms are relaxed at the sides, with slight outward angles.  In the second frame, the right arm moves upward and slightly forward, presenting the flower (imagined to be in the right hand). The wrist is angled slightly downward. The left arm remains mostly still, angled slightly outward. In the third frame, the right arm extends forward slightly further, almost as if offering the flower more directly. The wrist remains angled downward. The left arm might shift slightly inward for better balance. In the fourth frame, the right arm begins to return to its resting position, the motion slow and deliberate. The left arm remains largely still. In the fifth frame, both arms return to their initial relaxed positions.

frames.beat: arms rest at the sides, calm before the bloom
right arm gently lifts and extends, offering the flower
the flower is presented forward, a delicate offering
the right arm slowly retracts, the gesture complete
both arms return to a relaxed, resting state
